tactical_shape: Dynamic attacking players; carried menace but looked erratic and lacked composure
Pushed Scotland by attacking the gaps left in defense, but without seriously looking like scoring
standout_players: Johny Placide produced a fine save to deny Ché Adams
Jean-Ricner Bellegarde deflected McGinn's goal with an outstretched foot
home_read.momentum: Showed threat and pace, but the punditry frames them as lacking composure and not quite sharp enough to punish Scotland.

tactical_shape: Looked impressive in moments moving forward but left gaps for Haiti to attack
Used long balls/direct build-up at times, including Hanley launching a fine long ball to Ché Adams
Ben Gannon-Doak provided crucial attacking influence from the right/forward areas
standout_players: Scott McTominay came within the width of a post of scoring
Ben Gannon-Doak's attacking influence was crucial
John McGinn scored the winner, though it was described as a scuffed goal
Ché Adams was involved in the move and thought he had scored before McGinn's rebound
fatigue_injuries: Scott McTominay sat out training on Thursday with a stomach complaint but returned on Friday and was said to be in 'perfect' condition
away_read.momentum: Returned to the World Cup with a win, but the display was unconvincing and the narrative is that tougher tests against Morocco and Brazil will demand more.
